description_long: This object consists of a linear arrangement of six medium-sized pumpkins, each with a visible brown stem, connected by a single black rod or pole running through their stems. The pumpkins are uniformly spaced and appear to be identical in size and shape, with a classic round, slightly flattened form and a warm orange color. The surfaces of the pumpkins are smooth with subtle ribbing, and there are no visible carvings, facial features, or decorative accessories. The black rod gives the impression that these pumpkins are part of a decorative garland or string, possibly intended for festive urban displays. The overall construction suggests a lightweight, possibly hollow design, suitable for temporary installation in public or communal spaces. The pumpkins rest on a flat surface, and their arrangement is orderly and intentional.
description: A string of six orange pumpkins with brown stems, threaded on a black rod, forming a decorative garland. The pumpkins are smooth, uncarved, and evenly spaced.
description_view_0: Six pumpkins with brown stems are seen in a diagonal line, clearly showing the black rod connecting them.
description_view_1: A top-down view reveals the circular tops of all six pumpkins and the full length of the connecting rod.
description_view_2: The pumpkins are viewed from the opposite diagonal, highlighting the rod and the uniformity of the pumpkin shapes.
description_view_3: A slightly elevated side view shows the tops and stems of the pumpkins, emphasizing their spacing and alignment.
